baseline.claimBoundary: Baseline values are simulation-only and not measured chip performance.

budgetOptimizer.claimBoundary: Discrete optimizer over the local analytical error model; not a calibrated control optimizer.
limitations: The optimizer searches fixed 5% budget increments and does not model correlations.
Scores are engineering triage scores over simulated constraints, not hardware feasibility proof.

completionAudit.objective: Deep-Hardening V3 Max-Out: push the simulation-only OQP-HRM envelope beyond 704/352 to the largest honest stretch envelope while improving crosstalk, reflection, fusion, 1e-9 margins, timing, decoder evidence, corner/Monte-Carlo robustness, and claim guardrails.

promptToArtifactChecklist.description: Simulate up to the max local V3 stretch envelope: 760 physical modes mapped to 380 dual-rail logical qubits.
Evaluate 1e-8 and 1e-9 logical-error targets with larger code distances.
Run stress axes for loss, detector efficiency, dark counts, phase error, and feed-forward latency.
Compute 1e-9 operating margins that meet the hardening targets for loss, detector efficiency, phase, and feed-forward latency.
Compute combined operating profiles that budget loss, detector efficiency, dark counts, phase error, and feed-forward latency together.
Search optimized budget splits and select balanced, detector-relaxed, loss-relaxed, and latency-relaxed profiles.
Rerank coupler/MZI/truth-switch candidates for fusion success and process fidelity.
Reach >99.95% nominal fusion success and >=99.9995% nominal process fidelity without sacrificing fidelity for success.
Check raw truth-switch crosstalk <0.30% and reflection <0.008%.
Generate attempts/s, heralded events/s, and logical cycles/s estimates.
Tighten virtual S-parameter margins below 0.30% crosstalk and 0.008% reflection while preserving the foundry-data blocker.
Estimate a dense review-only max-qubit layout below 22 mm^2 with more than 45% total route-length reduction, while preserving tapeout blockers.
Separate the few-ns heralding/switch fast path from the full decoder and close the fast path below 1.3 ns.
Add full-decoder complexity evidence below 15 ns without claiming production decoder readiness or few-ns full decode.
gaps: Synthetic sample bounds cannot statistically prove 1e-9 logical error without far more samples.
Decoder latency is a deterministic complexity model, not measured software or hardware runtime.

promptToArtifactChecklist.description: Raise the 1e-9 uniform stress scale to >=1.0 and pass the combined/worst-case stress points.
Close the raw truth-switch simulation gap below 0.30% crosstalk and 0.008% reflection without using the compensated candidate.
Generate a multi-objective Pareto front for coupler, MZI, phase-shifter, and truth-switch candidates.
Run worst-case corner and deterministic Monte-Carlo robustness sweeps with sensitivity ranking.
gaps: Corner factors are deterministic Node Alpha surrogate factors, not process-corner PDK data.
Foundry S-parameter acceptance remains 0/4 until calibrated compact models are supplied.
Monte-Carlo samples are deterministic surrogate perturbations, not measured wafer/process distributions.
Pass fractions are regression evidence for this simulator only.

promptToArtifactChecklist.description: Verify guardrails against compensated truth-switch substitution, foundry S-parameter promotion, qubit inflation, and throughput inflation.
Map every new local improvement to the prototype-readiness gates and keep real-world blockers explicit.
gaps: measured source, detector, packaging, control, and calibration reports

promptToArtifactChecklist.description: Keep non-goals honest: no unbacked qubit inflation, no throughput inflation, no foundry/prototype/tapeout readiness claim.

controlTimingModel.claimBoundary: Fast-path timing closure is analytical. Full decoder runtime is separate and is not hardware-in-the-loop feed-forward verification.

decoderTimingSeparation.fastPathRole: heralding, table lookup, switch/phase command issue
decoderTimingSeparation.fullDecoderRole: batch syndrome graph decoding and correction-frame update

limitations: No measured detector, TDC, heralding logic, driver, or phase-settling latency is attached.
Passing rows are implementation targets for the ultra-fast path, not proof that control_ready is true.
Full decoder latency is reported separately and is not claimed to fit inside the few-ns feed-forward window.

decoderEvidence.claimBoundary: Toy analytical matching decoder evidence only; not a production decoder benchmark.
limitations: Synthetic sample bounds cannot statistically prove 1e-9 logical error without far more samples.
Decoder latency is a deterministic complexity model, not measured software or hardware runtime.

errorModel.claimBoundary: Deep-Hardening V3 uses an erasure-aware analytical simulator model; it is not hardware-calibrated threshold evidence.
errorModel.name: node_alpha_v3_erasure_aware_margin_model

nextValidation: install MEEP/MPB and rerun eigenmode-device-run
extract port S-parameters with MPB mode decomposition
repeat at resolution >= 16 and 3D geometry before GDS promotion

limitations: Analytical surrogate, not a MEEP/FDTD result.
Not foundry-calibrated and not a wafer measurement.
Only suitable for local ranking and sensitivity exploration.
Prototype readiness gates must continue to require FDTD, S-parameters, PDK, and lab evidence.
simulationModelChanges: MEEP is unavailable in this Node Alpha environment; this artifact is a deterministic analytical surrogate.
Surrogate terms are parameterized by coupling gap, coupling length, phase shift, and waveguide width.
MZI/phase-shifter acceptance is evaluated as a through-state low-leakage operating point; balanced-splitter tuning is a separate operating point.
Use only for ranking and sensitivity exploration; do not promote as FDTD, foundry, or lab evidence.

mzi.imbalanceRatioNote: Not an acceptance blocker for through-state MZI/phase-shifter operation; balanced splitting must be swept as a separate operating point.

limitations: The upgraded source/detector row is a sensitivity scenario, not measured hardware.
Candidate rankings are based on local Node Alpha/FDTD artifacts and surrogate reports.

candidateReports.claimBoundary: Simulation-only hardening candidate; not FDTD/MPB/foundry/wafer evidence.
Simulation-only hardening candidate; not FDTD/MPB/foundry/wafer evidence.
Simulation-only hardening candidate; not FDTD/MPB/foundry/wafer evidence.
Simulation-only hardening candidate; not FDTD/MPB/foundry/wafer evidence.

designChanges: raw truth-switch family narrowed to sub-0.25% crosstalk and sub-0.005% reflection without substituting a compensated row
fusion MZI candidate uses a V5 balanced low-leakage surrogate profile that improves fidelity before ranking success
virtual S-parameter margins use V5 apodized coupler and reference-arm balancing assumptions under expanded corner sweeps
threshold reference uses the hardened low-loss MZI candidate as a simulation-only device-error proxy
scaled layout envelope adds a V9 max-out pitch-floor review limit with fixed lower-bound pitches and package bank ceilings rather than foundry routing rules
package envelope uses source/detector superbank scans and serialized driver banks while preserving raw port/pad counts and review ceilings
limitations: The hardened rows are Node Alpha design projections. They must be rerun as FDTD/MPB and then replaced by foundry or wafer S-parameters.
Normalized usefulTransmission can exceed passive power because existing Node Alpha surrogate artifacts use source-bank-normalized useful flux; passive S-parameter checks remain separate.

thresholdReferenceDevice.claimBoundary: Simulation-only hardening candidate; not FDTD/MPB/foundry/wafer evidence.

coupler.claimBoundary: Hardened virtual compact-model row; foundry S-parameters remain unavailable.

mzi.claimBoundary: Hardened virtual compact-model row; foundry S-parameters remain unavailable.

phase-shifter.claimBoundary: Hardened virtual compact-model row; foundry S-parameters remain unavailable.

truth-switch.claimBoundary: Hardened virtual compact-model row; foundry S-parameters remain unavailable.

limitations: Consistency checks guard simulator outputs and claim boundaries; they are not external validation.

jointErrorBudget.claimBoundary: Synthetic joint operating profiles only; these are not measured environmental or hardware tolerances.
limitations: Budget splits are analytical planning profiles; they do not replace foundry process distributions.
Joint profiles assume error terms add independently as in the local threshold model.

maxQubitEnvelope.claimBoundary: Maximum qubits are bounded by the local review-only layout envelope, not hardware or foundry feasibility.

maxQubitNoGoMap.claimBoundary: No-Go rows are local stretch-envelope simulation boundaries; no foundry, DRC/LVS, package, or tapeout feasibility is implied.

pitchGuardrail.reason: Rows above the 760-mode stretch closure would require either below-floor pitch assumptions or a new optical package ceiling; both are left as no-go rather than silently promoted.

limitations: Monte-Carlo samples are deterministic surrogate perturbations, not measured wafer/process distributions.
Pass fractions are regression evidence for this simulator only.

sensitivityRanking.interpretation: crosstalk perturbations increase truthSwitchCrosstalkRatio fastest in the deterministic sample tails.
reflection perturbations increase truthSwitchReflectionRatio fastest in the deterministic sample tails.
phase perturbations increase logicalErrorRate1e9Distance161 fastest in the deterministic sample tails.
loss perturbations increase logicalErrorRate1e9Distance161 fastest in the deterministic sample tails.
detector perturbations increase logicalErrorRate1e9Distance161 fastest in the deterministic sample tails.
latency perturbations increase logicalErrorRate1e9Distance161 fastest in the deterministic sample tails.

limitations: Pareto dominance is computed over local candidate metrics and hardened analytical rows.
No-Go entries are simulator-family triage flags, not physical impossibility statements.

noGoMap.reason: At least one raw candidate passes V3 crosstalk/reflection targets.
Current local surrogate rows do not cross the raw V3 target without compensation.

operationalEnvelope.claimBoundary: Analytical margin budget only; not measured process, detector, or package tolerance.

errorModel.claimBoundary: Deep-Hardening V3 uses an erasure-aware analytical simulator model; it is not hardware-calibrated threshold evidence.
errorModel.name: node_alpha_v3_erasure_aware_margin_model

limitations: Margins are single-axis budgets unless explicitly stated; simultaneous errors must share the same budget.
The stress scenario remains a synthetic grid and is not hardware-calibrated.

prototypeGapReduction.claimBoundary: This report increases local simulation evidence only. Prototype readiness remains governed by real foundry, hardware, and lab gates.

inputPrototypeReadiness.highestPriorityBlocker: No foundry-calibrated S-parameter compact models are attached to core devices.

items.improvement: Virtual S-parameter acceptance, passivity, reciprocity, and energy-balance gates tightened.
Generic-SiPh V3 stretch max-qubit envelope calculated through 760 modes and 380 logical qubits.
Minimum joint stress reduction calculated for 1e-8 and 1e-9 targets.
Feed-forward stage budget and sub-5.6 ns simulated fast path added.
Toy matching-decoder complexity and latency evidence generated for threshold candidates.
Raw truth-switch gap quantified separately from derived reflection compensation.
Attempt, heralded-event, and logical-cycle upper bounds remain attached to the performance package.

remainingHardStops: measured source, detector, packaging, control, and calibration reports

limitations: Mode scaling is analytical; it does not create a DRC-clean larger GDS.
Area scaling is extrapolated from the current generic layout envelope when available.

bottlenecks: No experimentally calibrated source brightness and indistinguishability model.
No photon-number resolving detector timing and saturation model.
No resource-state factory schedule.
No multiplexing strategy for failed heralded events.

statistics.calibrationProtocol: repeat primitive shots with known input occupancy and record PNR coincidence matrix

No photon-number resolving detector timing and saturation model.
No resource-state factory schedule.
No multiplexing strategy for failed heralded events.

scaledLayoutEnvelope.claimBoundary: Dense generic-SiPh review envelope only; no foundry PDK, DRC, LVS, or scaled routed tapeout GDS is claimed.
limitations: The max-qubit row is an aggressive dense review envelope, not a DRC/LVS-clean generated foundry layout.
Fiber pitch adapters, thermal crosstalk, package keepouts, source/detector placement, and process rules remain generic.

maxQubitSearch.claimBoundary: Maximum qubits are bounded by the local review-only layout envelope, not hardware or foundry feasibility.

bankedIoEnvelope.claimBoundary: Banking/mux model is a packaging envelope only; no package drawing, probe card, or DRC/LVS closure is claimed.

dominantBlockers: foundry_pdk_missing: generic SiPh layers are not a foundry-locked layer map.
drc_not_run: no foundry DRC deck has been executed.
lvs_not_run: no optical/electrical LVS deck has been executed.

stressRecovery.claimBoundary: Stress recovery is a local analytical optimizer, not a measured process-control result.

errorModel.claimBoundary: Deep-Hardening V3 uses an erasure-aware analytical simulator model; it is not hardware-calibrated threshold evidence.
errorModel.name: node_alpha_v3_erasure_aware_margin_model

limitations: Uniform stress scaling is a planning number; real process errors are correlated and distribution-shaped.
Stress points are analytical budget checks and do not replace calibrated process, detector, reflection, crosstalk, or control distributions.

errorModel.claimBoundary: Deep-Hardening V3 uses an erasure-aware analytical simulator model; it is not hardware-calibrated threshold evidence.
errorModel.name: node_alpha_v3_erasure_aware_margin_model

limitations: Threshold results are analytical; no production decoder or hardware-calibrated noise model is used.
Stress scenarios are synthetic parameter grids, not measured source, detector, phase, or feed-forward distributions.

throughput.claimBoundary: Upper-bound simulated throughput; source repetition rate, detector dead time, driver settling, and decoder runtime are not measured.

derivedCompensatedCandidate.claimBoundary: Analytical performance hypothesis only; must be replaced by 2D/3D FDTD, MPB S-parameters, or wafer data.
derivedCompensatedCandidate.designChange: add balanced reference arm and destructive-reflection trim stage in front of the truth-switch cell

limitations: Raw closure uses available local candidates only; it does not create new 3D/MPB/foundry truth-switch evidence.
The derived compensated candidate remains a design proposal until rerun as FDTD/MPB or measured.

nextGeometrySearch.objective: Stress-test the raw truth-switch family below 0.25% crosstalk and 0.005% reflection without analytical reflection compensation.

derivedReflectionCompensatedCandidate.claimBoundary: Analytical performance hypothesis only; must be replaced by 2D/3D FDTD, MPB S-parameters, or wafer data.
derivedReflectionCompensatedCandidate.designChange: add balanced reference arm and destructive-reflection trim stage in front of the truth-switch cell

limitations: The reflection-compensated row is an analytical Node Alpha design proposal, not a raw FDTD/MPB accepted device.
This is a local evidence gap; it should trigger a narrower geometry search or real 3D/MPB extraction.

blockers: coupler: virtual model is not foundry or wafer calibrated.
mzi: virtual model is not foundry or wafer calibrated.
phase-shifter: virtual model is not foundry or wafer calibrated.
truth-switch: virtual model is not foundry or wafer calibrated.

limitations: Virtual acceptance is a stricter Node Alpha consistency gate, not a foundry-calibrated S-parameter claim.
Reciprocity/passivity metrics are accepted only at the compact-report level when no bidirectional Touchstone data exist.
Corner factors are deterministic Node Alpha surrogate factors, not process-corner PDK data.
Foundry S-parameter acceptance remains 0/4 until calibrated compact models are supplied.
